source.module: Core Django Fundamentals вЂ“ Data Modeling

topicTags: Core Django Fundamentals вЂ“ Data Modeling

content.problemStatement:
Let's create a simple Book model to understand how models work in Django. We'll create a model with basic attributes of a book and then use it to add a book to our database.

The project structure is as shown below:

```python
book_project  в†ђ Our Django project
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ manage.py
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ book_project/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ settings.py
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ urls.py
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ....
в””в”Ђв”Ђ book_app  в†ђ Our Django app
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ....
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ models.py       в†ђ We will define our database models here (e.g., Book)
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ views.py        
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ urls.py         
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ migrations/     в†ђ Auto-generated files to create/update tables
        в””в”Ђв”Ђ __init__.py

```

1. First, we create our Book model in the `models.py` file of our app as follows:

```python
from django.db import models

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    author = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    publication_year = models.IntegerField()
    isbn = models.CharField(max_length=13)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title
```
The above is similar to the SQL query as shown below:
```sql
CREATE TABLE book_app_book (               
-- Django names the table using: appname_modelname.
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, 
    -- Django automatically creates a primary key field called id
    title VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    author VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    publication_year INTEGER NOT NULL,
    isbn VARCHAR(13) NOT NULL
);
```
2. Make Migrations and Migrate
Run the below codes on the terminal
```python
cd book_project
python manage.py makemigrations
python manage.py migrate
```

3. Now, we will use this model we created to create a new book entry. We'll do this in the Django shell. \
**Do the following:**
 
- Access the Django shell by running the following in your terminal.
```python
python manage.py shell
```

- Create a new book: (Copy and paste the below code to the shell)

```python
from book_app.models import Book
Book.objects.all().delete()

new_book = Book(
    title="Django for Beginners",
    author="William S. Vincent",
    publication_year=2020,
    isbn="9781735467207"
)
new_book.save()
```

After running the above code, a new book entry will be created in your database. \
`Book.objects.all().delete()` \
This line in the above code deletes all existing Book entries from the database. We do this to start fresh and avoid duplicate entries or conflicts вЂ” especially if you run the code multiple times during testing or practice.

4. Retrieve this book  by its ISBN from the database and print its details. 
(Copy the below code & paste it on the shell. Notice the output you get after each line)

```python
book = Book.objects.get(isbn="9781735467207")
print(f"Title: {book.title}")
print(f"Author: {book.author}")
print(f"Publication Year: {book.publication_year}")
print(f"ISBN: {book.isbn}")
```
Your output will look like this:



**You can also click on RUN which will run all the above mentioned codes and show you the output**
